problem_statement: The proliferation of LLMs in software development produces a massive volume of software artifacts that cannot be comprehensively and scalably evaluated: human evaluation is costly and fatigue-prone while traditional automated metrics fail to capture nuanced quality; the paper surveys the LLM-as-a-Judge paradigm as an automated evaluation/oversight surrogate.
magnitude_evidence: A survey of SE researchers revealed that 84% agree that human evaluation is problematic, primarily due to time constraints, the need for specialized practical knowledge, and the high cost involved [8]
insufficiency_evidence: Findeis et al. [20] ... demonstrate that augmenting the LLM judge with a code execution tool increased its agreement with ground truth from below 42% to approximately 72% (i.e. unaided LLM judge agreement was below 42%); also 'even powerful models like GPT-4-turbo frequently misjudge code correctness' [Crupi et al. 11]
solution_mechanisms: agentic judges with external tools (Agent-as-a-Judge, CodeVisionary)
verification and reranking of candidate solutions
adversarial testing and fortification of judges
notes: Forward-looking SE 2030 systematic review of 42 primary studies plus vision/roadmap; the paper's own oversight subject is evaluation of LLM-generated artifacts rather than a single deployed mechanism.
